executive_summary: PLAN database-agent FR-1 RCA empirically CONFIRMED root cause as PATH D: AFTER UPDATE trigger sync_is_working_on_with_session on claude_sessions table fires when status flips to 'stale', clearing strategic_directives_v2.is_working_on=false + active_session_id=NULL (claiming_session_id preserved). LEAD risk-agent's 2 direct UPDATE probes had earlier disproven the original 'cascade-trigger overreach on sd_v2' hypothesis (PATH A) — 47 triggers enumerated on sd_v2, NONE clear claim cols on UPDATE. The 18-witness PAT-LEO-INFRA-WRITER-CONSUMER-ASYMMETRY-001 'claim columns cleared by .update()' framing was a coincident-timing illusion: enrichment scripts run >120s, the cron flips claude_sessions.status='stale' concurrently, the trigger clears sd_v2 claim cols, and operators saw their UPDATE land seconds later and (wrongly) attributed the clearing to their own write. cleanup_stale_sessions Block 3 then fires 30s later as SECONDARY clearer (releases all 3 cols). Fix surface: surgical CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION on sync_is_working_on_with_session so the clearing branch only fires on irrevocable transitions (sd_key→NULL, status→released/completed) NOT on recoverable 'stale' status. Defense-in-depth: 60s heartbeat keep-alive subprocess in sd-start.js prevents the cron from flipping status='stale' in the first place during long sub-agent execution. Plus regression-pin static guard on trigger body to detect future regressions. Closes feedback 18c57c39 + retroactively eliminates _restore-claim-*.mjs workaround pattern. ZERO backward-compat regressions per database-agent audit (cleanup_stale_sessions Block 3 still provides irrevocable 30s-grace-then-clear; handoff.js currently FAILS for in-flight sessions and Option 1 fixes this; sd:next/sd:status get extra 30s of is_working_on=true after stale flip — minor UX, still truthful). pg_cron NOT installed — external scheduler invokes cleanup_stale_sessions; trigger fix covers ALL invokers regardless of cron source.

functional_requirements.title: Surgical CREATE OR REPLACE on sync_is_working_on_with_session trigger function
functional_requirements.description: Modify the AFTER UPDATE trigger function on claude_sessions so the clearing branch fires ONLY on irrevocable transitions, NOT on recoverable 'stale' status. New conditional: IF (OLD.sd_key IS NOT NULL AND NEW.sd_key IS NULL) OR (OLD.status='active' AND NEW.status IN ('released','completed') AND OLD.sd_key IS NOT NULL) THEN clear claim cols on sd_v2 WHERE sd_key=OLD.sd_key AND active_session_id=OLD.session_id (CAS guard). Migration MUST end with NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema' in same transaction (gotcha PR #3691 W2). Backward-compat: cleanup_stale_sessions Block 3 (30s grace then irrevocable clear) UNAFFECTED — still provides backstop for genuinely stale sessions. Migration file: database/migrations/<date>_sync_is_working_on_preserve_recoverable_stale.sql. Idempotent CREATE OR REPLACE.
acceptance_criteria: Migration file exists at database/migrations/<date>_sync_is_working_on_preserve_recoverable_stale.sql
Migration uses CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION (idempotent)
Migration ends with NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema'
Post-migration empirical test: UPDATE claude_sessions SET status='stale' → sd_v2 claim cols UNCHANGED (PATH D fix verified)
Post-migration verify: UPDATE claude_sessions SET status='released' → sd_v2 claim cols CLEARED (irrevocable transition still works)
Post-migration verify: cleanup_stale_sessions Block 3 (30s grace) still clears all 3 cols on STALE_CLEANUP
pg_proc snapshot before/after committed for reference

functional_requirements.title: Heartbeat keep-alive subprocess in sd-start.js (defense-in-depth at SOURCE)
functional_requirements.description: Add heartbeat keep-alive subprocess that pings claude_sessions.heartbeat_at every 60s while sd-start session is active. Prevents the 120s cleanup_stale_sessions threshold from firing for in-flight LEO sessions. Fork detached child process via child_process.fork() that runs scripts/lib/heartbeat-keepalive.mjs in a loop with SIGHUP/SIGTERM handlers for clean exit. PID written to .claude/pids/heartbeat-<session-id>.pid. Auto-stops on session release. Eliminates the trigger CONDITION at the source (status never flips to 'stale' for active sessions) — orthogonal to FR-2 (which fixes the trigger BEHAVIOUR when condition does fire). Both layers needed: FR-2 covers cases where keep-alive crashes/exits; FR-3 covers FR-2 deploy-lag + protects pre-FR-2 sessions.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/lib/heartbeat-keepalive.mjs exists with fork-detach pattern
sd-start.js spawns keep-alive after successful claim
Keep-alive PID written to .claude/pids/heartbeat-<session-id>.pid
SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers gracefully exit + delete pidfile
Configurable interval via env LEO_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SEC (default 60)
Empirical test: claim SD, wait 150s, verify claude_sessions.status STILL 'active' (pre-fix would be 'stale')
On sd-start re-run: detect existing PID, kill stale keep-alive, spawn fresh

Pattern source: SD-FDBK-INFRA-MIGRATE-EMIT-FEEDBACK-001 PR #3693 dual-anchor static-pin. Mocking-independent.

system_architecture.overview: Database-side trigger surgical fix on claude_sessions.sync_is_working_on_with_session function (FR-2) addresses the actual root cause empirically reproduced by PLAN database-agent. App-side heartbeat keep-alive subprocess in sd-start.js (FR-3) addresses the trigger CONDITION at source (prevents status='stale' flip during long sub-agent execution). Defense-in-depth: 2 independent layers, both needed. FR-4 regression-pin static guard prevents future regressions to FR-2. FR-5 audit trail provides observability for future RCA + detection if FR-3 keep-alive fails.

components.name: sync_is_working_on_with_session (PG function, modified)
components.responsibility: AFTER UPDATE trigger on claude_sessions. Existing behaviour: clears sd_v2.{is_working_on, active_session_id} on ANY claude_sessions.status change. New behaviour: clears ONLY on irrevocable transitions (sd_key→NULL OR status→released/completed).
components.interactions: Receives OLD/NEW row context from claude_sessions UPDATE. Calls UPDATE strategic_directives_v2 SET ... WHERE sd_key=OLD.sd_key AND active_session_id=OLD.session_id (CAS guard). Adds INSERT INTO session_lifecycle_events for FR-5 audit.

components.name: heartbeat-keepalive.mjs (NEW Node subprocess)
components.responsibility: Long-running detached process. Pings claude_sessions.heartbeat_at via Supabase service-role every LEO_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SEC seconds (default 60). Eliminates cleanup_stale_sessions trigger condition for active LEO sessions.
components.interactions: Spawned by sd-start.js via child_process.fork({detached:true, stdio:'ignore'}).unref(). PID written to .claude/pids/heartbeat-<session>.pid. Listens for SIGHUP/SIGTERM for clean exit. Re-spawning sd-start kills existing keep-alive PID and spawns fresh.

components.name: session_lifecycle_events (NEW table)
components.responsibility: Append-only audit log of session status transitions. Each row: id, session_id, sd_key, event_type, old_status, new_status, claim_cleared (bool), payload jsonb, created_at.
components.interactions: Written by FR-2 trigger. Read by future RCA tooling + dashboards. Indexed on (session_id, created_at) and (sd_key, created_at).

components.name: verify-claim-col-preservation.mjs (NEW post-merge smoke)
components.responsibility: Live DB end-to-end verification of FR-2 fix. Runs ~3min after deploy.
components.interactions: Uses Supabase service-role + scripts/lib/supabase-connection.js::createDatabaseClient. Spawns probe session row, claims probe SD, simulates stale flip, asserts preservation, cleans up.

system_architecture.data_flow: Long-running session: sd-start.js claims SD → forks heartbeat-keepalive subprocess → claims persist. cleanup_stale_sessions cron checks heartbeat_at every interval — for active LEO session with FR-3 keep-alive, heartbeat is fresh → status STAYS 'active' → trigger NEVER fires the clearing branch. Edge case (FR-3 keep-alive crashed): cron flips status='stale' → FR-2 trigger fires → checks transition type → 'active'→'stale' is recoverable → claim cols PRESERVED → operator can recover by reactivating session. Irrevocable case (sd_key set to NULL OR status→released/completed): trigger clears claim cols as before. cleanup_stale_sessions Block 3 (30s grace then irrevocable clear) UNAFFECTED — still provides backstop after 30s for genuinely abandoned sessions.
integration_points: scripts/sd-start.js — calls fork() to spawn heartbeat keep-alive after successful claim_sd RPC
database/migrations/<date>_sync_is_working_on_preserve_recoverable_stale.sql — applied via supabase CLI or sandbox migration runner; sandbox-blocked path covered by FR-8 smoke runner post-merge
.github/workflows/post-merge-cascade-fix-verify.yml (NEW) — runs FR-8 smoke runner ~3min after PR merge to main
scripts/lib/supabase-connection.js — used by heartbeat keep-alive + smoke runner for service-role access

implementation_approach: EXEC sequence: (1) Write migration file FR-2 + FR-5 in same transaction (CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION + CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS session_lifecycle_events + NOTIFY pgrst). (2) Write static-pin tests FR-4 against the migration file. (3) Write FR-3 heartbeat-keepalive.mjs + sd-start.js fork integration. (4) Write FR-3 unit tests TS-9/10/11. (5) Write FR-6 integration tests TS-1..5+12 (skipped if sandbox-blocked, run in post-merge). (6) Add canonical-write-paths.md registry row FR-7. (7) Update CLAUDE_LEAD.md with disproof lesson. (8) Wire FR-8 post-merge smoke runner in .github/workflows. (9) Apply migration via sandbox runner if available; FR-8 covers post-merge if not. Pattern source for heartbeat keep-alive: scripts/lib/lifecycle/sd-claim-reaper.mjs detached-fork pattern. Pattern source for static-pin: SD-FDBK-INFRA-MIGRATE-EMIT-FEEDBACK-001 PR #3693. Pattern source for CAS UPDATE: PR #3691 partial UNIQUE INDEX precedent.

integration_operationalization.consumers: Primary consumer: every Claude Code session running LEO Protocol (sd-start, handoff.js, sub-agent invocations). Currently affected by intermittent claim loss when sub-agent runs >120s. Post-fix: long-running enrichment + sub-agent sessions complete without _restore-claim workaround. Secondary consumer: dashboards reading claude_sessions.status (sd:next, sd:status, coordinator inbox) — get +30s window of is_working_on=true after stale flip (still truthful per database-agent backward-compat audit).

dependencies.name: claude_sessions table (upstream)
dependencies.failure_mode: If table dropped or status enum changes, FR-2 trigger fails — but this is impossible without coordinated migration

dependencies.name: strategic_directives_v2 table (downstream)
dependencies.failure_mode: If active_session_id column dropped, CAS guard breaks — extremely unlikely; documented in FR-2 migration comments

dependencies.name: session_lifecycle_events table (NEW, downstream)
dependencies.failure_mode: If table not created, trigger INSERT fails silently (EXCEPTION handler in trigger body)

dependencies.name: scripts/sd-start.js (upstream caller of FR-3)
dependencies.failure_mode: If sd-start crashes before fork, keep-alive not spawned — FR-2 trigger still preserves claim cols (defense-in-depth)

dependencies.name: external scheduler invoking cleanup_stale_sessions (upstream)
dependencies.failure_mode: If scheduler stops, no cleanup at all — orthogonal to this SD

integration_operationalization.data_contracts: FR-5 introduces session_lifecycle_events: (id BIGSERIAL PK, session_id UUID, sd_key TEXT, event_type TEXT, old_status TEXT, new_status TEXT, claim_cleared BOOL, payload JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()). Indexes: (session_id, created_at), (sd_key, created_at). FR-2 modifies sync_is_working_on_with_session function body — no schema change. .claude/pids/heartbeat-<session>.pid files (FR-3) — text files containing OS PID, lifecycle managed by keep-alive subprocess.
integration_operationalization.runtime_config: Env vars: LEO_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SEC (default 60, FR-3 keep-alive). LEO_HEARTBEAT_KEEPALIVE_DISABLED (default false, FR-3 disable for tests). Feature flags: NONE (migration is one-shot CREATE OR REPLACE; trigger fix applies for everyone after migration runs). Deployment sequence: (1) Apply FR-2 migration to live DB (sandbox or post-merge). (2) Merge PR (FR-3 keep-alive starts protecting new sessions immediately). (3) Run FR-8 smoke runner ~3min post-deploy.
integration_operationalization.observability_rollout: FR-5 audit trail (session_lifecycle_events) provides per-trigger-fire observability. FR-8 smoke runner emits [SD_CLAIM_COL_PRESERVATION_VERIFIED] OR [SD_CLAIM_COL_PRESERVATION_DEGRADED] audit marker post-merge. Rollout: incremental (FR-2 migration is idempotent, can re-run; FR-3 keep-alive opt-out via env var). Rollback: revert FR-2 migration via reverse CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION (restoring original trigger body) + git revert PR; FR-3 keep-alive auto-stops on session release. Monitoring: SELECT count(*) FROM session_lifecycle_events WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour' GROUP BY claim_cleared — should show ZERO claim_cleared=true rows for active sessions, all such rows should be tied to released/completed transitions.

risks.description: FR-2 trigger modification breaks an undiscovered consumer
risks.mitigation: PLAN database-agent enumerated 16 functions referencing claim cols + ZERO regressions identified. Backward-compat: cleanup_stale_sessions Block 3 unchanged. handoff.js currently FAILS for in-flight sessions — Option 1 fixes this (positive change). Migration is CREATE OR REPLACE (idempotent + reversible).
FR-8 post-merge smoke runner verifies live DB behaviour ~3min after deploy. PR ships migration source; merge → external scheduler / Supabase Dashboard applies; smoke runner asserts.

risks.description: PostgREST schema-cache miss after CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION
risks.mitigation: Migration ends with NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema' in same transaction (PR #3691 W2). Post-deploy verify via SELECT prosrc FROM pg_proc.

risks.description: FR-3 heartbeat keep-alive subprocess crashes / orphans
risks.mitigation: FR-2 trigger fix is the PRIMARY safety net — even if keep-alive fails, trigger preserves claim cols on stale flip (recoverable transition). Defense-in-depth. PID file lifecycle prevents stale process accumulation.
Defer retention policy to follow-up SD. FR-5 trigger uses minimal payload. If size becomes issue: add periodic VACUUM + retention policy (e.g., DELETE WHERE created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days').

risks.description: Detached fork pattern incompatible with Claude Code sandboxing
risks.mitigation: Test in EXEC: spawn keep-alive from sd-start, verify .pid file written, verify ps -p shows live process. If incompatible: fall back to in-process setInterval (less robust but functional). Document in FR-3 acceptance criteria.

risks.description: FR-3 races with cleanup_stale_sessions: keep-alive ping arrives microseconds after cron flip → session stuck stale
risks.mitigation: Trigger CAS guard (active_session_id = OLD.session_id) ensures cross-session safety. Keep-alive includes status='active' UPDATE alongside heartbeat_at refresh — explicitly resets stale flag if cron beat us to it.

risks.description: Two-phase risk-agent at PLAN catches BLOCKER class only after PRD locked (PR #3691 PA-1 lesson)
risks.mitigation: Risk-agent v2 invocation MANDATORY after this PRD is finalized but BEFORE PLAN-TO-EXEC handoff. Specifically re-checks FR-2 SQL literal against pg_proc evidence.

risks.description: EXEC scope creep: migration of existing _restore-claim scripts requested
risks.mitigation: PRD scope explicit: existing one-offs OUT of scope (Q8 deletion audit ~60% reduction). FR-2 retroactively eliminates the workaround NEED for FUTURE sessions. Existing _restore-claim-emit-feedback.mjs becomes dead code after merge — can be deleted in follow-up cleanup SD.

risks.description: FR-3 conflicts with sd-claim-reaper.mjs (existing detached process)
risks.mitigation: Different PID files (.claude/pids/heartbeat-* vs .claude/pids/reaper-*). Different responsibilities. Verified via code review of scripts/lib/lifecycle/.

deployment_strategy: Single PR ships migration + code in atomic unit. Migration applied via Supabase CLI / Dashboard / sandbox runner pre-merge if possible; otherwise FR-8 post-merge smoke runner verifies ~3min after merge. No staged rollout needed — fix is backward-compat per database-agent audit. Rollback path: git revert PR + reverse CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION migration.
